messages.content:
```
  File "/tmp/ipykernel_546993/1563466032.py", line 39, in filter_nonjsonl_split_lang_first_level_theme
  File "/tmp/ipykernel_546993/1563466032.py", line 20, in predict
  File "/share/envs/haijun-kernel/lib/python3.10/site-packages/fasttext/FastText.py", line 239, in predict
    return labels, np.array(probs, copy=False)
ValueError: Unable to avoid copy while creating an array as requested.
If using `np.array(obj, copy=False)` replace it with `np.asarray(obj)` to allow a copy when needed (no behavior change in NumPy 1.x).
For more details, see https://numpy.org/devdocs/numpy_2_0_migration_guide.html#adapting-to-changes-in-the-copy-keyword.
```

This is a **NumPy 2.0 compatibility break** hitting `fasttext`’s Python wrapper: it calls `np.array(probs, copy=False)`, which now can raise `ValueError` when NumPy can’t honor “no copy”.

### Fix options (pick one)

#### 1) Easiest: downgrade NumPy to 1.x
```bash
pip install "numpy<2"
# or
conda install "numpy<2"
```
This is the most reliable fix if you don’t control the `fasttext` package code.

#### 2) Upgrade/patch `fasttext` (if a NumPy-2-compatible release exists)
Try:
```bash
pip install -U fasttext
```
If your `fasttext` version is still not compatible, you’ll need one of the next options.

#### 3) Patch the installed `fasttext` wrapper locally (quick hotfix)
Edit the file mentioned in the traceback:
`.../site-packages/fasttext/FastText.py` around the `predict` method and replace:
```python
np.array(probs, copy=False)
```
with:
```python
np.asarray(probs)
```
(or just `np.array(probs)`).

This aligns with NumPy’s migration guide suggestion.

#### 4) Workaround in your code (least preferred; may be clunky)
If you can avoid calling `fasttext`’s `predict` that returns probabilities as NumPy arrays, you could call a variant that doesn’t trigger the conversion—but usually the wrapper always does this conversion, so this often won’t help.

---

If you tell me:
- your `numpy` version (`python -c "import numpy; print(numpy.__version__)"`)
- your `fasttext` version (`pip show fasttext`)
- whether you use pip or conda

…I can recommend the best exact command sequence for your environment.
